proteins: Pork shoulder (7-8 lbs) - for pulled pork
Beef chuck roast (4 lbs) - for stew, cut into 1.5" cubes
Chicken thighs, bone-in (5-6 lbs) - for roasting
Eggs (5 dozen) - 36 hard boiled + 24 for her daily breakfast (3/day)
Bacon (2 lbs) - 1.5 lbs pre-cooked in air fryer + 0.5 lb raw for bacon-wrapped chicken
Salmon fillet (1.5 lb) - fresh cook mid-week (him 250g, her 125g = 375g × 2 days)
Steak, ribeye or strip (1.5 lb) - fresh cook end of week (him 300g, her 150g)
vegetables: Broccoli (3 heads) - roasted, served with multiple meals
Brussels sprouts (3 lbs) - roasted, in plates and bowls
Cauliflower (1 large head) - for cauliflower mash only
Spinach (3 bags) - salads + sautéed with steak
Mixed greens (3 containers) - for 8 salad bases
Celery (2 bunches) - 4 stalks for stew, rest for snacking
Radishes (1.5 lbs) - low-carb potato substitute in stew
Green cabbage (1 small or 1/2 large) - for slaw
fresh: Cherry tomatoes (3 pints) - Mediterranean mix, salads
Fresh thyme (1 bunch) - 6 sprigs for stew
dairy: Butter (8 oz / 0.5 lb) - 4 tbsp mash + 2 tbsp salmon + 2 tbsp steak
Cheese, variety (1 lb) - Cobb toppings, snack plates
Heavy cream (8 oz / 1 cup) - 1/4 cup for mash + extra
Greek yogurt, full-fat (64 oz) - her daily 150g + snacks
Cottage cheese (2 × 16oz tubs) - her daily 200g supplement
pantry: Beef broth (32 oz) - 4 cups for stew
Tomato paste (1 small can) - 3 tbsp for stew
Mayonnaise (1 jar) - 3 tbsp slaw + 3 tbsp deviled eggs
Almonds or mixed nuts - yogurt topping

notes.removed: Fresh rosemary (not used in any recipe)
notes.reduced: Cauliflower 3→1, Lemons 6→3, Butter 1.5lb→0.5lb, Feta 12oz→6oz, Heavy cream 1qt→1cup, Beef broth 64oz→32oz

overview.concept: Shop Sunday morning, cook 12-4pm, done for the week

steps.title: START - Big Proteins Go In
do: Preheat OVEN #1 to 300°F
Preheat OVEN #2 to 400°F

PORK: Pat 7-8 lb shoulder dry. Rub with 3 tsp salt, 1.5 tsp each pepper, garlic powder, paprika, cumin. Place in dutch oven or roasting pan, add 3/4 cup water, cover tightly with foil or lid. Into OVEN #1.

BEEF STEW: Cut 4 lb chuck into 1.5" cubes. Season with salt and pepper. Add to SLOW COOKER #1 with: 4 cups beef broth, 3 tbsp tomato paste, 2 tbsp Worcestershire, 3 bay leaves, 6 thyme sprigs. Set to HIGH.
CHICKEN: Pat 5-6 lbs thighs VERY dry. Toss with 3 tbsp olive oil, 3 tsp salt, 1.5 tsp each pepper, garlic powder, paprika. Arrange on 2 sheet pans skin-side up.
steps.status_after: Pork braising in Oven #1, Beef stew in slow cooker, Chicken prepped

steps.title: Chicken In, Eggs On
do: CHICKEN into OVEN #2 (both sheet pans, staggered racks) - needs 45 min total
EGGS: Place 36 eggs in large pot (may need 2 batches), cover with cold water by 1 inch. Bring to rolling boil on stovetop.
Set timer for chicken: 45 min
steps.status_after: Pork braising, Beef stewing, Chicken roasting, Eggs heating

steps.title: Eggs Rest, Bacon Starts
do: EGGS: Once boiling, cover pot, remove from heat. Set timer for 12 min.
BACON: Load 1.5 lb into AIR FRYER (may need 2 batches). 400°F for 10-12 min (check at 10).
steps.status_after: Pork braising, Beef stewing, Chicken roasting, Eggs resting, Bacon crisping

steps.title: Eggs to Ice Bath, Bacon Done
do: EGGS: Transfer to ice bath. Let sit 5 min, then peel.
BACON: Remove from air fryer, set on paper towels to cool.
Add STEW VEGGIES to slow cooker: 4 stalks celery (chunked), 12 oz mushrooms (halved), 1.5 lb radishes (halved). Stir.
steps.status_after: Pork braising, Beef stew has veggies now, Chicken roasting, Eggs cooling, Bacon done

steps.title: Prep All Vegetables
do: While chicken finishes, prep all roasting veggies:
Cut broccoli into florets
Halve brussels sprouts
Slice zucchini into 1/2" rounds
Cut bell peppers into strips
Cut cauliflower into florets (keep separate - this gets boiled)
Peel eggs as they become cool enough to handle

steps.title: Chicken Out, First Veggies In
do: CHICKEN: Remove from OVEN #2. Check internal temp 175°F. Set aside to rest.
Toss BROCCOLI & BRUSSELS with 2 tbsp olive oil, 1 tsp salt, 1/2 tsp garlic powder.
BROCCOLI & BRUSSELS into OVEN #2 - needs 30 min. Set timer.
steps.status_after: Pork braising (1 hr in), Beef stewing (1 hr in), Chicken resting, Broccoli/Brussels roasting

steps.title: Start Cauliflower, Toss Veggies
do: CAULIFLOWER: Add florets to large pot, cover with water. Bring to boil, cook 12-15 min until very tender.
Toss broccoli & brussels in oven at halfway point.
Toss ZUCCHINI & PEPPERS with 2 tbsp olive oil, 1 tsp salt, Italian herbs. Ready for next oven round.
steps.status_after: Cauliflower boiling, First veggies almost done

steps.title: Veggie Swap, Make Mash
do: BROCCOLI & BRUSSELS: Remove from OVEN #2. Set aside.
ZUCCHINI & PEPPERS into OVEN #2 - needs 25 min.
CAULIFLOWER: Drain VERY well (squeeze out water!). Add to food processor with 4 tbsp butter, 1/4 cup heavy cream, 1 tsp salt, 1/2 tsp garlic powder. Process until smooth.
steps.status_after: Pork braising (1.5 hr in), Zucchini/Peppers roasting, Mash done

steps.title: Zucchini Out, Fresh Prep
do: ZUCCHINI & PEPPERS: Remove from OVEN #2.
FRESH PREP:
- Wash greens and spinach, spin dry, store with paper towel
- Slice cucumber for snacking
- Cut remaining celery into sticks
- Rinse berries, dry gently
SLAW: Thinly slice 1/2 cabbage head. Toss with 3 tbsp mayo, 1 tbsp red wine vinegar, salt/pepper. Store in container - improves overnight.
DRESSING: Shake together 3/4 cup olive oil + 1/4 cup red wine vinegar + 1 tsp Dijon + 1 minced garlic + salt/pepper
steps.status_after: All veggies done, Fresh items prepped

steps.title: Check Beef Stew
do: BEEF STEW: Check tenderness. Beef should be getting tender but not falling apart yet.
Taste broth, adjust salt if needed.
Remove bay leaves and thyme stems.
Continue cooking - about 45 more min.

steps.title: Pork Out, Shred Everything
do: PORK: Remove from OVEN #1. Should be fork-tender after 3 hours. Shred with two forks. Mix with pan juices.
BEEF STEW: Should be done - meat tender, vegetables soft. Turn to WARM.
START PORTIONING (2x system: Blue lid = Him 2x, Pink lid = Her 1x):
- Pulled pork: 250g (2x) or 125g (1x) per container
- Chicken: 350g (2x) or 175g (1x) per container
- Eggs: keep in carton, portion when assembling
- Crumble bacon: 50g (2x) or 25g (1x) portions
- Roasted veggies: 200g (2x) or 100g (1x) portions
- Cauliflower mash: 200g (2x) or 100g (1x) portions
steps.status_after: All proteins shredded, Starting to portion

steps.title: Assemble Grab-and-Go Meals
do: BEEF STEW: Ladle into containers - 600g (2x/Blue) or 300g (1x/Pink)
PULLED PORK PLATES: 2x = 250g pork + 150g slaw + 200g peppers + 50g bacon | 1x = 125g + 75g + 100g + 25g
CHICKEN PLATES: 2x = 350g chicken + 200g mash + 200g broccoli | 1x = 175g + 100g + 100g
PORK POWER BOWLS: 2x = 250g pork + 200g zuc + 200g brus + 2 eggs | 1x = 125g + 100g + 100g + 1 egg
Label containers: Blue = 2x (Him), Pink = 1x (Her)

steps.title: Prep Fresh Add-On Containers & Snacks
do: DEVILED EGGS (24): Halve 12 eggs, mash yolks with 6 tbsp mayo + 2 tsp Dijon + pinch salt. Fill whites, dust with paprika. Store in container.
SALAD BASES: 150g greens (2x/Blue) or 75g (1x/Pink) - keep dressing separate
MEDITERRANEAN MIX (2): Small containers with cucumber + tomatoes + olives + feta - add to chicken or greens
COBB TOPPINGS (2): Small containers with bacon + egg + cheese - add to salad base + protein
AVOCADOS: Keep whole, slice day-of: 100g (2x) or 50g (1x)

steps.title: Organize Fridge & Clean Up
do: TOP SHELF: Grab-and-go meals (beef stew, pork plates, chicken plates, power bowls)
MIDDLE SHELF: Fresh add-on containers, salad bases, dressing
BOTTOM: Raw proteins for fresh cooking (salmon, steak), avocados
DOOR: Dressing, pickles, hot sauce, condiments
CLEAN UP: Wash pots, pans, sheet pans
steps.status_after: DONE! 17 grab-and-go meals + deviled eggs + salad kits + fresh cook options

timeline_summary: 12:00 - Pork in Oven #1, Beef stew in slow cooker, Prep chicken
12:15 - Chicken in Oven #2, Start eggs on stovetop
12:25 - Eggs rest off heat, Bacon in air fryer
12:35 - Eggs to ice bath, Bacon done, Add stew veggies
12:40 - Prep all vegetables while chicken finishes
1:00 - Chicken out, Broccoli & Brussels in Oven #2
1:15 - Cauliflower on stovetop, Toss roasting veggies
1:30 - First veggies out, Zucchini in, Make cauliflower mash
1:55 - Zucchini out, Prep fresh items, Make slaw & dressing
3:00 - Pork out & shred, Beef stew done, Start portioning
4:00 - Make deviled eggs, prep fresh add-on containers

results.portion_system: Standard containers - Him grabs 2, She grabs 1
grab_and_go: Pulled Pork Plates × 3 (Him: 6 meals, Her: 3 meals)
Chicken Thigh Plates × 3 (Him: 6 meals, Her: 3 meals)
Pork Power Bowls × 3 (Him: 6 meals, Her: 3 meals)
fresh_containers: Salad bases × 6 (greens ready for Cobb, Med, Breakfast salads)

broccoli_100g.usage: Chicken Plates (6) + Pork Plates (3) + Salmon (3) + Bacon Chicken (3)

bacon_25g.usage: Pork Plates (3) + Cobb (3) + Breakfast (3) + Bacon Chicken (3)

eggs_whole.usage: Deviled (12) + Power Bowls (6) + Cobb (3) + Breakfast (6) + Salmon (3) + Her breakfast (21)

grab_and_go.name: Beef Stew

weekly_servings.note: Tue, Thu meal1 + Sat as Stew+Salad

grab_and_go.instructions: Microwave 2-3 min or heat on stovetop until hot.

grab_and_go.name: Pulled Pork Plate
grab_and_go.instructions: Microwave 2-3 min. Slaw is fine cold or warm. Add pickles, hot sauce.

grab_and_go.name: Chicken Thigh Plate
grab_and_go.instructions: Microwave 2-3 min, or reheat chicken in 400°F oven 10 min to re-crisp skin. Add butter to mash.

grab_and_go.name: Pork Power Bowl
grab_and_go.instructions: Microwave 2-3 min. Slice avocado fresh, add hot sauce.

add_fresh.name: Cobb Salad
add_fresh.instructions: Combine salad base with toppings. Add sliced avocado and protein. Drizzle dressing.

add_fresh.name: Mediterranean Chicken Salad
add_fresh.grab: Salad base + Mediterranean mix + chicken from fridge
add_fresh.instructions: Combine greens with Mediterranean mix. Warm chicken or serve cold. Add dressing.

add_fresh.name: Breakfast Salad
add_fresh.grab: Salad base + bacon + eggs from fridge
add_fresh.instructions: Warm bacon in pan to render fat. Toss greens in warm fat. Top with eggs (fried fresh or hard boiled), avocado. Hot sauce.

add_fresh.name: Beef Stew + Salad
add_fresh.instructions: Reheat stew. Dress salad. Hearty and satisfying.

cook_fresh.name: Pan-Seared Salmon
cook_fresh.instructions: Season salmon with salt/pepper. Heat oil in pan over medium-high. Sear skin-down 4 min, flip, cook 3 min more. Warm broccoli in microwave. Plate salmon with broccoli, eggs, butter, lemon, capers.

cook_fresh.name: Steak Dinner
cook_fresh.instructions: Let steak come to room temp (15 min). Season heavily with salt/pepper. Heat cast iron until smoking. Sear 4 min per side for medium-rare. Rest 5 min, top with butter. Sautee spinach in steak pan drippings. Warm broccoli. Plate.

cook_fresh.name: Bacon-Wrapped Chicken
cook_fresh.instructions: Wrap each thigh with 2 strips bacon. Bake at 400°F for 35-40 min until bacon is crispy and chicken reaches 175°F. Warm broccoli. Serve with ranch.

snacks.name: Deviled Eggs
snacks.instructions: Halve eggs, mash yolks with mayo and mustard. Fill whites, dust with paprika.

snacks.name: Cheese & Veggie Plate
snacks.instructions: Slice cheese, arrange with veggies and olives.

snacks.name: Greek Yogurt + Berries
snacks.instructions: Top yogurt with berries and nuts.

snacks.name: Bacon & Avocado
snacks.instructions: Top avocado halves with crumbled bacon and seasoning.

days.morning_prep: Take salmon out 15 min before cooking
Take steak out 15 min before cooking
days.her_note: Steak day - skip cottage cheese, steak has plenty of protein

days.morning_prep: Wrap chicken in bacon for evening cook
days.her_note: Breakfast salad has eggs - skip breakfast eggs
days.note: Prep next week while bacon chicken bakes
